W07-Reflection Essay-Introduction to Positron

Author

Andrew Nguyen

Published

Invalid Date

1 Introduction

In this workshop, I learned about Positron, which is a newer development environment for coding and data science. The goal of this module was to install Positron, explore how it works, and understand how it compares with RStudio. I also learned about how artificial intelligence tools like GitHub Copilot can be integrated into the coding environment to help with programming tasks.

Overall, this workshop helped me understand how Positron can improve the workflow when working on data science or coding projects.

2 Starting a Positron Project

2.1 Prompt

Start a new project and Quarto file (.qmd) inside Positron.

2.2 Response

For this part of the assignment, I first created a folder on my computer called Intro-Positron inside my course folder. I used my computer’s file manager to organize the files so that everything related to this workshop is stored in the same place.

After creating the folder, I opened Positron and created a new project using that folder as the project directory. Once the project was created, Positron automatically generated a .posit folder that stores the project settings.

Next, I created a Quarto file (.qmd) and saved it inside the project folder. This file allows me to combine written explanations, formatting, and code in the same document. One thing I noticed is that Quarto makes it easier to organize reports because everything can be written and rendered into an HTML page.

3 Quarto Formatting Features Used

In this report I tried to use several formatting tools that Quarto provides so the report looks organized and professional.

Some of the features used include:

  • Headings and section organization
  • Lists and bullet points
  • Blockquotes
  • Callout blocks
  • Table of contents
  • Section numbering

Using Quarto makes it easier to organize a report because the structure is built directly into the document.

Tip

Quarto is useful because it allows you to combine writing, formatting, and code all in the same document.

4 Positron vs RStudio

4.1 Prompt

Based on what you learned from Step 1 and Step 3, what do you like about Positron compared with RStudio?

4.2 Response

After watching the videos and trying Positron myself, I noticed a few differences compared with RStudio.

One thing I like about Positron is that the interface feels more modern and cleaner. It reminds me a little of Visual Studio Code, which makes it easier to navigate between files and tools.

Another thing I noticed is that Positron seems to focus more on integrating modern development tools, especially artificial intelligence features. This makes coding faster because the editor can help suggest code or help explain errors.

RStudio is still a very powerful program for statistical analysis, but Positron feels like it is designed for a more modern data science workflow.

5 Using AI Inside Positron

5.1 Prompt

Describe the various ways you can use AI inside Positron.

5.2 Response

One interesting part of this workshop was learning how AI tools can be used inside Positron. These tools can help programmers write code more efficiently and reduce mistakes.

For example, AI can help by:

  • Suggesting code while typing
  • Completing functions automatically
  • Explaining what certain code does
  • Helping debug errors
  • Generating documentation

Some AI tools are free, while others require a paid subscription depending on the service. These tools can be especially helpful for beginners who are still learning programming syntax.

6 AI Tools Installed

6.1 Prompt

Which AI tools have you installed or set up? Which AI tools did you find beneficial?

6.2 Response

One of the AI tools introduced in this workshop was GitHub Copilot. This tool acts as an AI coding assistant that can generate code suggestions directly in the editor.

I applied for the GitHub Student Developer Pack, which gives students free access to GitHub Copilot. After applying and being accepted, I connected Copilot to my development environment.

Figure 1 below shows the confirmation screenshot of my acceptance into the GitHub Student Developer program.

6.3 Prompt

Play with GitHub Copilot and describe whether you find it helpful or distracting.

6.4 Response

After trying GitHub Copilot for a while, I found it to be mostly helpful. It suggests lines of code while you type, which can make coding faster and reduce the chances of syntax errors.

For example, when writing simple functions or data manipulation code, Copilot often predicts what I am trying to write and completes it automatically. This can save time and make the workflow smoother.

However, sometimes the suggestions appear when I already know what I want to write, which can feel slightly distracting. In those situations, it is easy to ignore the suggestions and continue typing.

Overall, I think GitHub Copilot is a useful tool, especially for learning and improving coding efficiency.

Note

AI tools should be used as helpers, but it is still important to understand the code yourself.

7 Publishing the Report to GitHub Pages

7.1 Prompt

Publish this report to GitHub Pages and provide the URL.

7.2 Response

After completing the report, I uploaded the project files to a GitHub repository and used GitHub Pages to publish the HTML report as a website.

GitHub Pages allows users to host static websites directly from their repositories, which makes it easy to share reports or projects online.

GitHub Pages URL:

https://education.github.com/learner/learn

8 Conclusion

Overall, this workshop helped me become more familiar with Positron and its features. I learned how to create projects, use Quarto to generate reports, and integrate AI tools such as GitHub Copilot.

Compared with RStudio, Positron feels more modern and better integrated with new development tools. The AI features were also interesting because they can help make coding faster and easier.

As development environments continue to evolve, tools like Positron and AI assistants will likely become more common in data science and programming workflows.
